economic_notes: Nation has significant business enterprises including seafood operations (TFN Seafoods), resort (Tin Wis Resort, Tsawaak RV Resort), micro-hydro power projects (Haa-ak-suuk Creek, Winchie Creek, Canoe Creek Hydro), and construction management (Hithuiis Spirit). Total combined long-term debt of business entities approximately $53.3M. Post year-end, Council approved $8.211M FNFA financing to support refinancing of Tsawaak RV Resort LLP debt.
notes: ISC funding flows through Nuu-chah-nulth Tribal Council (NTC); the $9,879,812 NTC line is treated as ISC funding per Note 15. Total government transfers calculated as NTC ($9,879,812) + CMHC ($807,956) + Province of BC ($584,943) + FNHA ($313,827) + DFO ($163,700) + BC Gaming ($967,232) = $12,717,470. Nation business entities (GBEs) are accounted for using modified equity method and reported a combined loss of $1,555,158 in 2024 vs earnings of $323,882 in 2023. Tangible capital assets net book value not directly stated on face of balance sheet in extracted text. Net debt end of year was $3,471,602 (net debt position). Consolidated entities include Operating Fund, Trust Fund, Social Housing Fund, Enterprise Fund, Treaty Fund, and Capital Fund. No schedule of remuneration was included in the extracted text. Community and Human Services ($3,914,580) includes both social and health; Natural Resources ($1,625,714) reported separately. Adopted PS 3400 Revenue standard prospectively April 1, 2023.
